items.prompt: adds and subtracts bounded rational numbers across fraction, decimal, and signed representations
items.expected_evidence: add and subtract fractions, decimals, and signed rational quantities using defensible structure
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner computes the result accurately while preserving quantity meaning.

items.prompt: explains how common units, equivalent forms, or signed magnitude justify a rational-number addition or subtraction result
items.expected_evidence: explain why the computed result is reasonable using magnitude, benchmark, or direction language
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the operation using coherent magnitude and sign structure.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to representation_alignment_breakdown.
items.expected_evidence: convert or align representations when needed without losing quantity meaning
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies how the peer broke common-unit or equivalent-form structure.

items.prompt: succeeds across symbolic, number-line, verbal, and simple contextual rational-number addition and subtraction cases
items.expected_evidence: connect symbolic work to number-line movement and bounded contextual situations
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves rational-operation meaning across multiple representations and contexts.
